paṭiggāhako.gram: masculine agent noun from paṭiggaṇhāti, nominative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to take, to grasp” (lengthened to gāh).

paṭighasanna.meaning: perception of ill will; a mind tinged with anger
breakdown: As printed; representing saññā “perception” in the compound paṭighasaññā.
paṭighasanna.note: uncertain: the form as printed (sanna) most likely stands for saññā “perception” (paṭighasaññā, perception of aversion).

paṭijānamāno.gram: present participle of paṭijānāti, nominative masculine singular
breakdown: Present stem of √ñā “to know”: “to acknowledge, to claim”.

paṭijānanti.meaning: they confess; they acknowledge; they claim
paṭijānanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of paṭijānāti (paṭi + √ñā)

paṭikkhipanena.gram: neuter action noun from paṭikkhipati, instrumental singular
breakdown: Prefix “back, against”; the k doubles at the boundary.
Root meaning “to throw”: “to throw back”, refuse.
Prefix “back, against”; sandhi doubling of k.
Root meaning “to throw”: “to throw back”, refuse.

paṭikkhittassa.meaning: of what has been rejected, refused
paṭikkhittassa.gram: past participle of paṭikkhipati, genitive/dative singular

paṭikkhittā.gram: past participle of paṭikkhipati, nominative plural (or feminine singular)

paṭikkhittāni.gram: past participle of paṭikkhipati, neuter nominative/accusative plural

paṭikoṭeyya.meaning: would beat back; would thrash, push back

paṭilābho.gram: masculine noun from paṭilabhati, nominative singular
breakdown: Nominative singular ending of the action noun.

paṭinissaggo.gram: masculine noun from paṭinissajjati, nominative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to let go, to release” (sajj > sagg before the noun suffix).
paṭinissaggo.goenka: Paṭinissaggo, relinquishment or total letting go, appears in the description of ānāpānassati and of the path's culmination: by observing reality with equanimity, the meditator gradually gives up all clinging. It is part of the stock sequence — viraga (fading away), nirodha (cessation), paṭinissagga (relinquishment) — that describes how attachment to the aggregates dissolves through practice rather than through mere belief.

paṭinissajjeyya.meaning: would forsake; should renounce, give up
breakdown: Root meaning “to let go, to release”.

paṭipadā.meaning: way of practice; the means of reaching a destination; course of conduct
breakdown: Root meaning “to step, to go”: the stepping-along, the way one walks to the goal.
paṭipadā.goenka: Paṭipadā is the practical way of walking the path — not a doctrine to be accepted but a course of practice to be undertaken step by step. The middle way taught by the Buddha is majjhimā paṭipadā, and the way leading to the cessation of suffering is dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā, the Fourth Noble Truth, which each meditator must actually walk to reach the destination.

paṭipadāya.meaning: of/for the way of practice; of the means of reaching the goal; conduct
breakdown: Way of practice, course leading to the goal.

paṭipajjanti.meaning: they enter upon (a path); they practise, follow the way
paṭipajjanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of paṭipajjati (paṭi + √pad)
breakdown: Present stem of √pad “to step, to go”.

paṭipanno.meaning: practising; entered upon (the path); conducting oneself
paṭipanno.gram: past participle of paṭipajjati, nominative masculine singular

paṭipatta.meaning: wrong practice; (in the compound micchāpaṭipatta-adhikaraṇahetu) by reason of wrong conduct
paṭipatta.gram: past participle of paṭipajjati, in compound
breakdown: Practised, conducted (past participle stem of paṭipajjati).
In consequence of (in the source compound).
paṭipatta.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line carries the whole compound micchāpaṭipatta-adhikaraṇahetu “by reason of wrong practice”; only the participle stem belongs to this headword.

paṭipatti.meaning: practice; correct conduct; (in the compound sammāpaṭipatti-adhikaraṇahetu) by reason of right practice
breakdown: Abstract noun suffix: the actual walking of the path, practice.
paṭipatti.goenka: Paṭipatti is actual practice, as distinguished from pariyatti, theoretical knowledge of the texts. The tradition constantly emphasises that the Dhamma must be practised and experienced, not merely studied or believed: it is sammāpaṭipatti, right practice, that brings the results the suttas describe.

paṭippassambheyya.meaning: would be calmed; would subside, be quieted
breakdown: Prefix “back”; the p doubles at the boundary.
Prefix “forth”; sandhi doubling of s follows.

paṭipucchābyākaraṇīyo.meaning: (a question) to be answered by a counter-question
breakdown: To be explained, to be answered (future passive participle of byākaroti).

paṭipūjenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of paṭipūjeti (paṭi + √pūj)
breakdown: Stem of pūjeti “to honour, to venerate”.
Root meaning “to cling, to hide”: withdrawing into solitude.

paṭisantharati.meaning: welcomes; receives kindly, is favourably disposed towards
breakdown: Root meaning “to spread”: “to spread out (a welcome) before someone”.
paṭisantharati.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (paṭissutvā “agree, consent”); only “welcome, be favourably disposed” belongs to paṭisantharati.

breakdown: Root meaning “to discern, to declare”: to reflect thoroughly.

paṭisañcikkhato.gram: present participle of paṭisañcikkhati, genitive/dative singular
breakdown: Stem of paṭisañcikkhati “to reflect, to consider”.
Genitive/dative singular of the present participle (-ant stem).
paṭisañcikkhato.note: divergence: the lexicon marks the form “p.p.”, but it is the genitive of the present participle (DPD); the sense “reflecting, considering” is unchanged.

paṭisaṁhareyyāsi.meaning: you should withdraw; you should remove, take back
paṭisaṁhareyyāsi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular optative of paṭisaṁharati (paṭi + saṁ + √har)
breakdown: Root meaning “to carry”: “to carry back”, withdraw.
Root meaning “to ward off, to forbid”.

paṭisedhana warding.gram: glossary artifact; neuter action noun paṭisedhana
paṭisedhana warding.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for paṭisedhana “warding off, refusal”.

paṭisotagāmiṁ.summary: This compound describes the Dhamma (or its follower) as “going against the stream” of craving and worldly habit.

paṭissutvā.meaning: having agreed; having consented, having assented
breakdown: Prefix “back, in reply”; sandhi doubling of s.
Root meaning “to hear”: “to hear in reply”, to assent.

paṭivedhataṁ.meaning: (in the compound) hard to penetrate; the quality of being difficult to comprehend
paṭivedhataṁ.gram: noun in compound (duppaṭivedha + taṁ), from the stock description of the Dhamma as deep, subtle, hard to see and hard to penetrate
breakdown: Root meaning “to pierce”: paṭivedha is penetration, comprehension.
Ending as printed; the full source compound is gambhīra + nipuṇa + duddasa + duranubodha + sukhuma + duppaṭivedha — deep, subtle, hard to see, hard to understand, fine, hard to penetrate.
paṭivedhataṁ.note: uncertain: the headword is a fragment of the long stock compound describing the Dhamma; the lexicon's breakdown covers the whole compound.

paṭividdhaṁ.gram: past participle of paṭivijjhati (paṭi + √vidh + ta), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to pierce”: to penetrate with understanding.

paṭivinodeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of paṭi + vi + √nud
breakdown: Root meaning “to push, to drive”: the causative means “to drive out, dispel”.

paṭiviratassa.meaning: of one abstaining (from); of one who has rejected
paṭiviratassa.gram: past participle of paṭiviramati, genitive/dative singular
breakdown: Root meaning “to delight”: virata, “turned away from delighting in”, abstaining.

paṭiyādenti.meaning: they prepare; they provide, make ready
paṭiyādenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of paṭiyādeti
breakdown: Stem related to √yat “to arrange, to prepare”.

paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ.meaning: acting upon what has been admitted; proceeding according to acknowledgement (a method of settling disciplinary issues)

breakdown: Causative stem of √ṭhā “to stand”: “cause to stand”, establish.

paṭṭhapeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of patiṭṭhahati

pecca.meaning: thereafter; after death; having passed on (from this world)
pecca.gram: indeclinable; gerund of peti (pa + √i) used adverbially
breakdown: Root meaning “to go”: “having gone on”, i.e. after this life.
Root meaning “to see, to look” (pa + ikkh > pekkh).

pesesi.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular of peseti

pesuññamakāsi.meaning: engaged in backbiting; uttered slander (literally “made slander”)
pesuññamakāsi.gram: compound verb phrase, aorist: pesuññaṁ + akāsi
breakdown: The final niggahīta joins the following vowel as m.

petakiccatthāya.meaning: for the purpose of the duties towards the deceased (funeral rites)
breakdown: Duty, what is to be done.
For the purpose of, for the sake of (dative).
petakiccatthāya.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (pettivisaya “the realm of the unhappy ghosts”); only “for the purpose of duties to the deceased” belongs to this headword.

phalasamāpattisukhatthāyāti.meaning: “for the sake of the happiness of the attainment of fruition” (with quotation marker)
phalasamāpattisukhatthāyāti.gram: compound, dative singular + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Quotation marker; ā + ti fuse at the boundary (atthāyāti).

pharusa vācā.goenka: Pharusā vācā, harsh speech, is one of the four kinds of wrong speech to be abandoned in sammāvācā (right speech), alongside lying, slander and idle chatter. Abstaining from harsh words is part of the training in sīla on which samādhi and paññā are built.
